decks.notes: The main goal of this deck is to ramp up mana quickly using cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple, allowing the player to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker. The deck also includes control elements like Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's strategy. By ramping up mana and playing high-impact threats, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and control the game, leading to victory through powerful Eldrazi creatures and efficient control spells.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient creatures and disruptive spells to put pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan. It aims to control the board with cards like Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler while steadily building a threatening board presence with creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also has versatile removal options like Prismatic Ending and Consign to Memory to deal with a variety of threats. It looks to win the game quickly through aggressive plays and resource denial.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while using spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to remove opponent's threats. The deck also relies on card advantage and manipulation with cards like Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble. It aims to apply pressure on the opponent early in the game while maintaining a steady stream of card advantage to close out the game quickly.

This deck uses a combination of creatures with various abilities and a toolbox approach to achieve its goal. By utilizing cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Green Sun's Zenith, the deck aims to assemble powerful combinations that can control the board and generate value. Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Malevolent Rumble provide additional removal and disruption, while cards like Endurance and Strangleroot Geist offer resilience and protection. The deck's synergy and flexibility allow it to adapt to different situations and opponents, making it a versatile and unpredictable threat in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. It includes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. The deck also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to deal with opposing threats. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory provides additional support and buffs for your creatures. The deck aims to quickly establish board presence and maintain pressure to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By utilizing powerful cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, along with removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude, the deck aims to apply pressure while keeping the opponent on the back foot. Cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory provide additional disruption and tempo advantage, allowing the deck to maintain control of the game and secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, and Urza's Tower) to ramp up mana quickly and play powerful colorless Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Sire of Seven Deaths. It also utilizes control elements like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and All Is Dust to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Karn, the Great Creator is included to tutor for key artifacts like Relic of Progenitus and Vexing Bauble. Overall, the deck aims to control the game with a mix of disruption and powerful threats while leveraging the mana advantage provided by UrzaTron.

This deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate large amounts of colorless mana to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. It aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker, while also using cards like Devourer of Destiny and Kozilek's Command to maintain board presence. The deck also utilizes artifact synergies and disruptive elements to control the flow of the game and overwhelm the opponent.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing a mix of creatures, spells, and enchantments to quickly deal damage and control the board. The key focus is on efficiently using cards like Lightning Bolt, Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, and Territorial Kavu to constantly apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of Leyline of the Guildpact and Arena of Glory helps to support this aggressive strategy by boosting the power of your creatures and protecting them from removal spells. Overall, this deck thrives on speed, aggression, and disruption to secure victory.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to efficiently use creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal damage early in the game, while also controlling the board with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Seasoned Pyromancer. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional support for your creatures, while Arena of Glory and Elegant Parlor help to boost your team's power and toughness. With a mix of fast creatures and direct damage spells, this deck aims to win quickly and decisively.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Allosaurus Rider, Eldritch Evolution, and Neoform to quickly tutor for and put into play high-impact creatures like Griselbrand and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The deck also includes protection in the form of Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer, as well as ramp and card filtering to help set up the combo. The key strategy is to efficiently search for and play key combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately leading to a potential lethal attack.

The deck focuses on applying early pressure with aggressive creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. It includes cards that can answer a variety of threats and control the board, allowing the player to maintain tempo and pressure their opponent. The deck also includes flicker effects to generate value from enter-the-battlefield abilities of creatures like Quantum Riddler and Solitude. Overall, it aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggression and control elements, known as Death and Taxes archetype in Modern format.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage until it can assemble the combo of Scapeshift and Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle to deal a lethal amount of damage to the opponent. Key cards like Ice-Fang Coatl and Coiling Oracle help to draw cards and generate value, while disruption like Force of Negation and Lightning Bolt keep the opponent's game plan in check. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent before winning with a powerful combo finish.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble a powerful combo without relying on lands. By using cards like Goblin Charbelcher, Lotus Bloom, and Whir of Invention, the deck aims to create a winning combo quickly and efficiently. Cards like Disrupting Shoal and Force of Negation help protect the combo pieces, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Thundertrap Trainer provide card advantage and additional utility. Overall, this deck focuses on creating a strong and consistent win condition through a unique and landless approach in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and control the board with spells like Kozilek's Command and Ugin's Labyrinth. By using cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Promised End, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats while disrupting their game plan. It also includes land destruction with cards like Ghost Quarter and World Breaker to further disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and overpower the opponent with its Eldrazi creatures and spell effects.

The key idea of this deck is to quickly generate mana with cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion, while setting up a combo with Ral, Monsoon Mage and cards like Glimpse the Impossible and Grapeshot to deal massive damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes Past in Flames to recast spells from the graveyard and Wrenn's Resolve to protect your combo pieces. Overall, the deck aims to create a strong synergy between card draw, mana generation, and combo potential to overwhelm and defeat your opponent as quickly as possible.
